reasoning: Without access to web search tools and given the knowledge cutoff constraints, I rely on the historical base rate. The question defines a recession strictly as two consecutive quarters of negative GDP growth initially reported within the 2025 calendar year. There are only three such combinations possible in 2025 (Q1-Q2, Q2-Q3, Q3-Q4). Historically, a technical recession occurs roughly once every 6-10 years, and the probability of it falling perfectly within a single calendar year is about 10-15%. Therefore, I estimate a 12% probability.
